neighborhoods.name: Downtown / Square
neighborhoods.description: College town center with Texas State University, San Marcos River, and vibrant square entertainment
neighborhoods.character: College town, river recreation, lively square

housing.predominantType: Apartments, student housing, and older homes

neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of San Marcos. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living San Marcos

neighborhoods.name: Kyle / Buda
neighborhoods.description: Southern communities between San Marcos and Austin with rapid growth and family amenities

housing.predominantType: Newer single-family homes and master-planned communities

neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to San Marcos's center and services.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living San Marcos

neighborhoods.name: Lockhart / Luling
neighborhoods.description: Eastern communities with BBQ capital heritage (Lockhart), very affordable housing, and small-town charm

housing.predominantType: Older houses, ranch homes, and modest properties

neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living San Marcos

neighborhoods.name: New Braunfels / Gruene
neighborhoods.description: German heritage town with Schlitterbahn waterpark, Gruene Hall, and Guadalupe River tubing

housing.predominantType: Single-family homes and Hill Country properties

neighborhoods.character_notes: Family-friendly area with good schools, parks, and community amenities. Popular with active retirees and families.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living San Marcos
